campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on September 2, 2025, to review the city's financial reports for July, including how the city's investments performed and the overall treasurer's status. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including walking, biking, and public transit routes.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan workplan (walking, biking, transit improvements)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to gather community input on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the standard first reading process to speed things up, with plans to finalize it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived to expedite approval
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and to authorize a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting covered routine administrative approvals and a construction project, though specific details about the construction work are unclear from the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from multiple March 2026 sessions
Authorization of construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Closed session discussions (specific topics not detailed in agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for late April. Council members reviewed which items should be added to the April 21st and April 28th meeting agendas, decided if any topics needed specific time slots, and checked paperwork requirements. They also reviewed official records from early April.
keyTopics: Finalized April 21, 2026 meeting agenda items and scheduling
Added new items to April 28, 2026 draft agenda
Set specific time slots for priority agenda items
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion
Reviewed official city records from April 2-9, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on major construction and infrastructure projects. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Capital improvement priorities for 2026-27

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review previous meeting notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing new regulations for the Residential Tenant Protections Program to update Chapter 19.71 of the city's municipal code.
keyTopics: Tenant protection policy changes being introduced
Review of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
Updates to residential tenant rights regulations
Housing and Human Services Commission business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were given a chance to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections for Chair and Vice Chair positions and started discussing the budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents can address the council on any topic
Chair and Vice Chair elections held
Fiscal year budget development presented
State law limits council decisions on items not formally on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to sue or legally challenge it, you have 90 days from when the decision becomes final to file your lawsuit, unless a different deadline applies.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to file lawsuits against city decisions
Legal challenge procedures for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements
Resident rights regarding city decision appeals

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and potentially approve changes to the city's long-term housing and community development plan for 2025-2030, and discussed updates to how the council fills vacant seats. The meeting also covered routine items like approving previous meeting minutes and planning the calendar for upcoming council meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year housing and community development funding plan
Possible changes to how council vacancy positions are filled
New work projects requested by individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is requesting permission to give the historic building a higher protection status and wants to add 5,000 square feet of extra space as a reward for rehabilitating the building and making it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for rehabilitation work
Seismic safety improvements to existing structure
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
